note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/NumberField/InfinitePlace/TotallyRealComplex.lean:47 — class IsTotallyReal (K) [Field K] : all infinite places of K are real; docstring states exactly this definition.
verify_note: Confirmed at that exact line: docstring 'field K is totally real if all of its infinite places are real, i.e. image of every K→ℂ embedding lies in ℝ' matches Wikidata Q2997826 description verbatim in substance; genuine exact formalization.

note: Mathlib/Combinatorics/SimpleGraph/Connectivity/EdgeConnectivity.lean:35 — def IsEdgeConnected (G k) := ∀ u v, G.IsEdgeReachable k u v, i.e. G is k-edge-connected.
verify_note: Confirmed: IsEdgeReachable is defined as remaining reachable after deleting any edge set of size <k, and IsEdgeConnected requires this for all vertex pairs — matches Wikidata Q3034655 'graph that remains connected whenever fewer than k edges are removed' exactly.

note: Mathlib/Topology/MetricSpace/Pseudo/Defs.lean:432 — def sphere x ε := {y | dist y x = ε}, generic in any (pseudo)metric space; the n-sphere is the specific case Metric.sphere (0 : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin (n+1))) 1, given manifold structure in Mathlib/Geometry/Manifold/Instances/Sphere.lean.
verify_note: Confirmed decl at that line; Wikidata Q306610 describes n-sphere as 'generalization of the ordinary sphere to arbitrary dimension' — the proposer's direction (decl is a generic ambient construction, n-sphere is the specific Euclidean instance) is honestly stated, not overclaimed as exact.

note: Mathlib/NumberTheory/ADEInequality.lean:231 — theorem classification (p q r : ℕ+) : 1 < sumInv {p,q,r} ↔ Admissible {p,q,r}, classifying solutions into A'/D'/E6/E7/E8 families; file docstring ties this directly to 'the classification of Dynkin diagrams, root systems, and semisimple Lie algebras' but this is the (p,q,r)-inequality classification, not a statement of the ADE Dynkin-diagram/singularity classification itself.
verify_note: Confirmed file/docstring content; theorem is about the p^-1+q^-1+r^-1>1 inequality's solution multisets, which are in ADE-labeled bijection with but not a formal statement of the Dynkin-diagram/Lie-algebra classification proper — 'related' at medium confidence is the honest call, not inflated to exact.

note: Mathlib/Data/List/PeriodicityLemma.lean:191 — theorem HasPeriod.gcd, the Periodicity Lemma / Fine-and-Wilf theorem for words (Lists): a word with periods p and q of length ≥ p+q-gcd(p,q) has period gcd(p,q). One classical result within the field, not a formalization of the field itself.
verify_note: Confirmed theorem statement matches note; Wikidata Q2985058 describes combinatorics on words as the broad study of words/sequences, and Fine-Wilf is one classical theorem in that field — proposer correctly avoided claiming this single lemma formalizes the whole field.
